description: DCHECK protects IsSavingAndFillingEnabled but is no-op in release; passwords saved when policy disables saving
fix_description: Replace DCHECK with if-return runtime check

description: CSP enforcement relies on error message string matching; frame removal causes different error string, bypassing CSP fallback block
fix_description: Check CSP policy proactively before fetch instead of relying on error message string

description: LNA permission check requires initiator to be in frame tree of navigating frame; opener-initiated cross-window navigation skips the check entirely
fix_description: Remove frame tree ancestor requirement, directly use initiator RFH for permission check

description: Opaque response validation only checks kCache source type, not kRaceNetworkAndCache which can also serve from cache
fix_description: Check actual_source_type instead of matched_source_type, or extend condition to cover kRaceNetworkAndCache when cache wins

description: should_be_filtered flag computed from real topic's domain set but inherited by random replacement topic, creating a binary oracle
fix_description: Set should_be_filtered = false for random topics, since random topics should always be returned regardless of caller

description: postMessage targetOrigin check at send time could theoretically be bypassed by TOCTOU if target navigates during PostTask delay
why_not_vuln: Two independent defense layers: IsCurrentlyDisplayedInFrame() detects navigation, and origin is re-checked at dispatch time. Both prevent cross-origin message delivery.

description: SAA StorageAccessHandle uses CreateFirstParty() for BroadcastChannel/SharedWorker, enabling cross-partition communication
why_not_vuln: This is the intended behavior of Storage Access API 'Beyond Cookies'. The API is specifically designed to grant unpartitioned storage access after user consent. CreateFirstParty() is correct here.

fix_description: N/A - team is aware, staged rollout in progress
why_not_vuln: Team is aware and actively rolling out. crbug.com/495999481 tracks this. Reporting disabled feature flags that are in staged rollout is not a valid VRP finding.

description: SharedWorker same_site_cookies field from renderer is not fully validated for first-party contexts
why_not_vuln: Exploiting this requires a compromised renderer process. Normal web pages cannot directly control Mojo IPC parameter values. The VRP attack model requires exploitation from an unprivileged web page.

description: Shared Storage selectURL timing difference between enabled and disabled cross-origin could leak state
fix_description: N/A - already mitigated by fake success response
why_not_vuln: Chrome explicitly returns fake success for disabled path to prevent timing side-channel. Both enabled and disabled paths resolve the JS Promise at similar timing. Not reliably exploitable.
